defs: The quality or state of being singular; some character or quality of a thing by which it is distinguished from all, or from most, others; peculiarity.
quotes: Pliny addeth this singularity to that soil, that the second year the very falling down of the seeds yieldeth corn.
I took notice of this little figure for the singularity of the instrument.
Your gallery Have we passed through, not without much content In many singularities.

defs: Possession of a particular or exclusive privilege, prerogative, or distinction.
quotes: No bishop of Rome ever took upon him this name of singularity [universal bishop].
Catholicism . . . must be understood in opposition to the legal singularity of the Jewish nation.
